channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says intel pause on Ukraine has been 'just about' lifted; says tariffs will make America rich
item.description: President Trump spoke to reporters on Air Force One on Sunday night, fielding questions about tariffs, Ukraine, Russia and retirement accounts experiencing a freefall.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's 'autopen signature' appears on most official docs, raising concerns over who controlled the WH: report
item.description: President Biden's signature on official executive documents shows the same autopen signature, renewing concerns over his mental acuity, a report shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its employees
item.description: Most of the 80,000 federal workers responsible for researching diseases, inspecting food and administering Medicare and Medicaid under the auspices of the Health and Human Services Department were emailed an offer to leave their job for as much as a $25,000 payment.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's transactional approach to diplomacy is a driving force on the world stage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump, the real estate developer turned commander in chief, is laying bare his style of diplomacy in the early weeks of his new term: It’s a whole lot like a high-stakes business deal, and his No. 1 goal is to come out of the transaction on top.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers consider allowing heavier ATVs
item.description: The ATV industry says consumer preferences are shifting toward larger side-by-side vehicles. Some environmental groups question whether heavier vehicles could cause more trail damage.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Facing competition from Big Tech, states dangle incentives and loosen laws to attract power plants
item.description: Facing spiking energy demand, U.S. states are pressing for ways to build new power plants faster as policymakers try to protect residents from higher electric bills and keep their economies on track.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House Republicans unveil bill to avoid shutdown and they're daring Democrats to oppose it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans unveiled a spending bill Saturday that would keep federal agencies funded through Sept. 30, pushing ahead with a go-it-alone strategy that seems certain to spark a major confrontation with Democrats over the contours of government spending.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump shifts tone on Musk as tensions rise with Cabinet
item.description: President Trump is signaling a softer approach to the mass firings of federal government workers led by Elon Musk as cracks emerge between the tech billionaire and Cabinet secretaries running the agencies he’s gutting.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Congress unveils spending plan after Trump calls on Republicans to avoid government shutdown
item.description: Republicans are unveiling a new plan to fund the government past the coming shutdown deadline next week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump has created a task force to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe's premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when his on-again, off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ras Baraka has highest favorability among Democratic candidates for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New polling from Fairleigh Dickinson University gave us some early insight on who voters are looking into in the race for New Jersey governor. Democratic voters weighed in on the candidates' favorability.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump order denies student loan relief to nonprofit workers engaged in 'improper' activity
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is ordering changes to the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program that would disqualify workers of nonprofit groups deemed to have engaged in “improper” activities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican lawmakers hold telephone town halls as protesters demand more
item.description: HOLLAND, Mich. (AP) — Protesters demanding an in-person town hall from their western Michigan GOP congressman chanted loudly Friday as honking drivers signaled support, disrupting the usual calm of a conservative family vacation town. Mere hours later, Rep. Bill Huizenga held a town hall — by phone.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: War heroes and military firsts are among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — References to a World War II Medal of Honor recipient, the Enola Gay aircraft that dropped an atomic bomb on Japan and the first women to pass Marine infantry training are among the tens of thousands of photos and online posts marked for deletion as the Defense Department works to
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, LOLITA C. BALDOR and KEVIN VINEYS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Students rally at Florida capitol opposing amendment petition reform
item.description: From the Panhandle to the Keys, hundreds of students from across Florida protested Thursday at the state capitol.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Hunter Biden asks for laptop data case to be dismissed, citing financial problems
item.description: “Plaintiff has suffered a significant downturn in his income and has significant debt in the millions of dollars range,” Hunter Biden’s attorneys wrote in a court filing.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: House votes to censure Texas Democrat Al Green for protest during Trump address
item.description: Texas Rep. Al Green, a Democrat, was officially censured Thursday in a vote in the House following his protest on Tuesday during President Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: NYC Council Speaker Adrienne Adams to run for mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams is joining the race for the mayor’s office, she's expected to announce on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis' State of the State: government accountability a key focus
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis gave his state of the state address Tuesday and offered his plan for the year ahead. Members of the legislature, and leaders past and present gathered in Tallahassee for the speech Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US tariffs on Canada and Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bondi says she was misled on Epstein documents
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says she was misled into believing she had all the files related to the Epstein investigations, and is now seeking additional information from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: The Education Department sent out an email to staffers on Friday saying if they quit by Monday at midnight, they will receive a $25,000 cash payout.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
